name: Cuernavaca

palette.name: Cortés Stone
palette.rationale: Warm ochre stone references the Palacio de Cortés and gives the palette a historic civic base.

palette.name: Borda Garden Green
palette.rationale: Garden green reflects the botanical and shaded garden identity around Jardín Borda.

palette.name: Cathedral Stucco
palette.rationale: A pale stucco neutral supports Cuernavaca's convent, cathedral, and historic-center walls.

palette.name: Rivera Mural Earth
palette.rationale: Earth red gives a restrained mural and regional-history accent without implying an exact pigment match.

palette.name: Fountain Blue
palette.rationale: Muted blue is an editorial cooling tone for fountains, water, and the city's garden-resort atmosphere.

sources.title: Centro Cultural Jardín Borda
sources.note: Used for garden, botanical, fountain, and cultural-center context.

sources.title: Museo Regional de los Pueblos de Morelos, Palacio de Cortés
sources.publisher: Instituto Nacional de Antropología e Historia
sources.note: Used for Palacio de Cortés and museum heritage context.

notes: Standard starter record; all colors are editorial interpretations of architecture, gardens, and cultural setting, not official municipal colors.
editorialSummary: Cuernavaca's starter palette layers warm historic stone, garden green, pale stucco, mural earth, and fountain blue into a calm city-of-gardens system for designers.
